recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Beef Taco Rice Salad Box

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 40 minutes

Meal role: lunch

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields British Lean Beef Steak Mince 5% Fat - 400g

### Carbs

- Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice - 150g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick Iceberg Lettuce - 110g
- Nature's Pick Cherry Tomatoes - 330g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack - 30g
- Solesta Vegetable Oil - 15ml
- Stonemill Ground Cumin - 4g
- Stonemill Smoked Paprika - 4g
- Nature's Pick Garlic - 6g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- mixing bowl
- small bowl
- 3 lunch boxes
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Rice cup: Weigh 150g long grain white rice. Used in Steps 1, 4 and 6.
- Beef pack: Keep 400g lean beef mince cold until the pan is hot. Used in Steps 2, 3, 4 and 6.
- Taco seasoning by hob: Mince 6g garlic. Keep cumin, smoked paprika and the 1 tbsp (15ml) oil by the hob to measure straight into the pan. Used in Step 3.
- Salad box: Shred 110g iceberg lettuce and chop 330g cherry tomatoes. Used in Steps 5 and 6.
- Lime dressing pot: Squeeze juice from 1 lime. Used in Steps 5 and 6.
- Pack setup: Set out 3 lunch boxes for the finished beef taco rice salad portions. Used in Step 6.

## Method

### 1. Cook the rice

Put the Rice cup into the saucepan with about 300ml water. Bring to a simmer, cover, cook for about 12 minutes, then turn the heat off and rest for 5 minutes with the lid on.

Uses:

- Long grain white rice - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 150g

### 2. Brown the beef

Heat the saute pan over medium-high heat. Add the Beef pack and spread it out. Cook for about 6-8 minutes, breaking it up, until the beef has browned patches and the watery steam has cooked off.

Uses:

- Mild taco beef mince - amount stated in this step: Ashfields British Lean Beef Steak Mince 5% Fat 400g

### 3. Bloom the taco seasoning

Use the Taco seasoning by hob. Add 1 tbsp (15ml) vegetable oil if the pan looks dry, then add the garlic, 2 tsp (4g) cumin and 2 tsp (4g) smoked paprika straight into the pan. Stir for about 1 minute until the garlic and spices smell cooked and coat the beef. Add a splash of water only if the spices catch.

Uses:

- Mild taco beef mince - amount stated in this step: Ashfields British Lean Beef Steak Mince 5% Fat 400g
- Mild taco beef seasoning - amount stated in this step: Solesta Vegetable Oil 15ml, Stonemill Ground Cumin 4g, Stonemill Smoked Paprika 4g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g

### 4. Cool the cooked rice and beef

Fluff the rice and spread the cooked beef and rice out separately for 10 minutes so they stop steaming before they go into the lunch boxes.

Uses:

- Mild taco beef mince - amount stated in this step: Ashfields British Lean Beef Steak Mince 5% Fat 400g
- Long grain white rice - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 150g

### 5. Prepare the salad and lime dressing

Use the Salad box and Lime dressing pot. Keep the lettuce and tomatoes crisp, and keep the lime dressing ready to spoon over the packed box.

Uses:

- Lettuce and tomato taco salad - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Iceberg Lettuce 110g, Nature's Pick Cherry Tomatoes 330g
- Lime dressing - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack 30g

### 6. Pack the beef taco rice salad boxes

Use the Pack setup. Spend about 3-4 minutes portioning the finished beef taco rice salad box by the saved serving element: Luke about 43%, Sarah about 33% and Louis about 24%. This is a finished-box share, so do not try to weigh cooked rice or cooked beef as raw ingredient amounts.

Uses:

- Mild taco beef mince - amount stated in this step: Ashfields British Lean Beef Steak Mince 5% Fat 400g
- Long grain white rice - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 150g
- Lettuce and tomato taco salad - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Iceberg Lettuce 110g, Nature's Pick Cherry Tomatoes 330g
- Lime dressing - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack 30g
- Mild taco beef seasoning - amount stated in this step: Solesta Vegetable Oil 15ml, Stonemill Ground Cumin 4g, Stonemill Smoked Paprika 4g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Finished beef taco rice salad box | about 43% of the finished beef taco rice salad box | about 33% of the finished beef taco rice salad box | about 24% of the finished beef taco rice salad box |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 562.2 kcal, 45g protein, 63g carbs, 15.5g fat, GBP 2.71
- Sarah: 431.4 kcal, 34.5g protein, 48.2g carbs, 11.9g fat, GBP 2.08
- Louis: 313.7 kcal, 25.1g protein, 35.1g carbs, 8.7g fat, GBP 1.53

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Cauliflower Cheese Soup With Buttered Sourdough Croutons

Creamy cauliflower cheese soup blended with cheddar, hard cheese and milk, finished with olive oil, black pepper and dried thyme.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 40 minutes

Yield: 3 soup bowls

Meal role: cheap family dinner

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Cowbelle Whole Milk 3.6% Fat - 500ml
- Emporium British Mature Cheddar Cheese - 150g
- Emporium Grated Hard Cheese - 80g
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 20g

### Fruit/Veg

- Nature's Pick British Cauliflower - 1 whole cauliflower
- Nature's Pick Brown Onions - 1 medium onion
- Nature's Pick Garlic - 3 small cloves

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 2 slices, about 120g total
- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 1 1/2 tbsp (22.5ml) total: 3/4 tbsp (11.25ml) for the onion base, plus 2 1/4 tsp (11.25ml) for finishing
- Ready, Set... Cook! Dried Thyme - 2 tsp (2g) total: 1 tsp (1g) in the cauliflower bowl, plus 1 tsp (1g) for finishing
- Morrisons Dijon Mustard - 5g
- Solesta White Wine Vinegar - 5ml

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 10 inch cast iron frying pan
- Nutribullet Smart Touch Blender
- Ladle
- Chopping board
- Knife
- Grater
- Spatula or wooden spoon

## Mise En Place

- Onion and garlic bowl: 1 medium onion chopped with 3 small garlic cloves sliced. Used in Step 1.
- Cauliflower bowl: 1 whole cauliflower cut into florets and small stalk pieces, with 1 tsp (1g) dried thyme. Used in Step 2.
- Cheese bowl: 150g grated cheddar, 80g hard cheese, 5g Dijon and 5ml white wine vinegar. Used in Step 4.
- Crouton bowl: 2 slices white sourdough diced with 20g butter ready by the cast iron pan. Used in Step 3.
- Serving setup: 3 soup bowls, 2 1/4 tsp (11.25ml) olive oil for finishing, 1 tsp (1g) dried thyme for finishing and the black pepper grinder. Used in Step 6.

## Method

### 1. Soften the onion and garlic

Heat the olive oil in the saute pan over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic with a pinch of salt. Cook for about 6-7 minutes until soft but not browned.

Uses:

- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 3/4 tbsp (11.25ml)
- Onion and garlic bowl - all

### 2. Simmer the cauliflower

Add the cauliflower, milk, water and dried thyme. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover loosely and cook for about 18-20 minutes until the cauliflower stalk pieces are soft.

Uses:

- Cauliflower bowl - all
- Cowbelle Whole Milk 3.6% Fat - 500ml
- Water - enough to help the liquid cover the cauliflower, about 200ml

### 3. Make the buttered croutons

While the soup simmers, melt the butter in the cast iron pan. Add the diced sourdough and cook for about 5-6 minutes, tossing until golden and crisp.

Uses:

- Crouton bowl - all: 2 diced sourdough slices and 20g butter

### 4. Add cheese and blend smooth

Take the soup off the heat. Stir in the cheese bowl while the soup is still hot so the cheese softens into the cauliflower base. Blend smooth in the Nutribullet jug for about 1-2 minutes, then return the soup to the saute pan.

Uses:

- Simmered cauliflower, milk, onion and garlic soup base - all
- Cheese bowl - all

### 5. Warm and season the soup

Warm the blended soup gently on low heat for about 2-3 minutes until glossy and hot enough to serve. Do not boil hard after the dairy goes in, because hard boiling can make cheese split or turn grainy. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

Uses:

- Blended cauliflower cheese soup - all

### 6. Serve the dinner bowls

Ladle the finished soup into 3 bowls. Add croutons, then finish each bowl with a measured olive oil circle, black pepper and dried thyme.

Uses:

- Cauliflower cheese soup - all
- Buttered sourdough croutons - all
- Serving setup - 3 dinner bowls, olive oil finish: Luke 1 tsp (5ml), Sarah 3/4 tsp (3.75ml), Louis 1/2 tsp (2.5ml); dried thyme finish: 1 tsp (1g) scattered across the bowls to match bowl sizes; black pepper

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Cauliflower Cheese Soup | 45% of the finished soup batch | 33% of the finished soup batch | 22% of the finished soup batch |
| Buttered Sourdough Croutons | 40% of the croutons | 35% of the croutons | 25% of the croutons |
| Olive Oil Thyme Finish | 1 tsp (5ml) olive oil + the largest thyme scatter | 3/4 tsp (3.75ml) olive oil + a medium thyme scatter | 1/2 tsp (2.5ml) olive oil + a small thyme scatter |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 941.3 | 49.6g | 50.6g | 61.6g | GBP 2.09 | 120.2 |
| Sarah | 714.6 | 37.0g | 40.0g | 46.1g | GBP 1.57 | 91.1 |
| Louis | 483.4 | 24.8g | 27.7g | 31.1g | GBP 1.07 | 61.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- The cheeses go into the hot soup before blending. This matches Luke's proven method and gives the smoothest texture.
- Blend the source-style soup volume once, then return it to the saute pan to warm gently.
- Keep the heat gentle after blending so the dairy stays smooth.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Cheddar Spinach Egg Bake Squares

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 25 minutes

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Carbs

- Nature's Pick British White Potatoes - 240g

### Vegetables

- Four Seasons Leaf Spinach - 120g

### Dairy

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 6 each
- Emporium British Mature Cheddar Cheese - 75g

### Pantry

- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 15ml

## Equipment

- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- single oven
- mixing bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Potato bowl: Dice 240g white potatoes into small cubes. Used in Steps 1, 3 and 4.
- Spinach bowl: Weigh 120g frozen leaf spinach. Used in Steps 2, 3 and 4.
- Egg and cheddar jug: Crack 6 eggs and grate 75g mature cheddar. Beat together just before baking. Used in Steps 3 and 4.
- Serve setup: Set up a board and 3 plates so the rested bake can be cut into 8 equal squares. Used in Step 4.

## Method

### 1. Parboil the potatoes

Add the Potato bowl to boiling water and simmer for about 6-7 minutes, until the cubes are just tender at the edges. Drain well so they do not water down the bake.

Uses:

- Parboiled white potato cubes - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick British White Potatoes 240g

### 2. Wilt the spinach

Heat the saute pan over medium heat. Add 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil, then the Spinach bowl. Cook for about 4-5 minutes, stirring now and then, until hot and the visible water has evaporated.

Uses:

- Wilted spinach base - amount stated in this step: Four Seasons Leaf Spinach 120g
- Olive oil for the pan - amount stated in this step: Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml

### 3. Bake the egg squares

Heat the oven to 180C. Spread the drained potatoes through the spinach in the saute pan, then pour over the Egg and cheddar jug. Bake for about 12-15 minutes, until just set in the centre and lightly golden on top.

Uses:

- Parboiled white potato cubes - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick British White Potatoes 240g
- Wilted spinach base - amount stated in this step: Four Seasons Leaf Spinach 120g
- Cheddar egg bake - all: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 6 each, Emporium British Mature Cheddar Cheese 75g
- Olive oil for the pan - amount stated in this step: Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml

### 4. Serve the egg bake squares

Use the Serve setup. Rest the bake for 5 minutes, then cut it into 8 equal squares. Serve Luke 3 squares, Sarah 3 squares and Louis 2 squares.

Uses:

- Finished serving elements - as shown in the Plate table

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Finished cheddar spinach egg bake squares | about 38% of the finished bake (3 of 8 squares) | about 38% of the finished bake (3 of 8 squares) | one quarter of the finished bake (2 of 8 squares) |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 413 kcal, 25.8g protein, 17.8g carbs, 27g fat, GBP 0.74
- Sarah: 413 kcal, 25.8g protein, 17.8g carbs, 27g fat, GBP 0.74

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Chicken Pasta Salad

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 25 minutes

Meal role: lunch

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets - 420g

### Carbs

- Everyday Essentials Penne Pasta - 130g
- Free From Glutenfree Fusilli - 70g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick Cucumber - 150g
- Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers - 160g
- Four Seasons Sweetcorn - 90g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Brooklea Greek Style Yogurt - 120g
- Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons - 25g
- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 15ml

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- 18 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- mixing bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Normal pasta pan: Weigh 130g normal penne for Luke and Louis. Used in Steps 1 and 5.
- Sarah pasta pan: Weigh 70g gluten-free fusilli for Sarah and keep it separate from the normal pasta. Used in Steps 1 and 5.
- Chicken board: Cut 420g chicken breast into bite-size pieces and keep it separate from salad vegetables. Used in Steps 2 and 5.
- Salad vegetable bowl: Dice 150g cucumber and 1 mixed pepper. Thaw or quickly rinse 90g sweetcorn and drain it well. Used in Steps 3 and 5.
- Lemon yoghurt dressing bowl: Mix 120g Greek yoghurt, juice from 1/2 lemon and 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil. Used in Steps 2, 4 and 5.
- Serve setup: Set out one large mixing bowl for Luke and Louis, one separate bowl for Sarah, and 3 lunch bowls for serving. Used in Step 5.

## Method

### 1. Cook the pasta branches

Cook the Normal pasta pan and Sarah pasta pan in separate saucepans of boiling water until tender, usually about 10-12 minutes. Drain them separately, rinse briefly to cool, then keep Sarah's pasta separate from the normal pasta.

Uses:

- Normal penne pasta for Luke and Louis - all: Everyday Essentials Penne Pasta 130g
- Sarah gluten-free fusilli pasta - all: Free From Glutenfree Fusilli 70g

### 2. Sear the chicken

Heat the saute pan over medium-high heat. Add 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil straight to the pan, then add the Chicken board. Cook for about 7-8 minutes until the chicken is browned in places and cooked through. Take it off the heat and let it cool for a few minutes before mixing into the salad.

Uses:

- Lemon chicken pieces - all: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 420g
- Lemon yoghurt dressing - amount stated in this step: Brooklea Greek Style Yogurt 120g, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 25g, Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml

### 3. Build the salad vegetables

Put the Salad vegetable bowl together and make sure the sweetcorn is drained well so the pasta salad does not turn watery.

Uses:

- Cucumber, pepper and sweetcorn salad mix - all: Nature's Pick Cucumber 150g, Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 160g, Four Seasons Sweetcorn 90g

### 4. Make the lemon yoghurt dressing

Mix the Lemon yoghurt dressing bowl until smooth. Keep it cold and spoonable; do not heat it.

Uses:

- Lemon yoghurt dressing - amount stated in this step: Brooklea Greek Style Yogurt 120g, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 25g, Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml

### 5. Fold and serve the two pasta salads

Use the bowls from the Serve setup. In the large bowl, fold the normal pasta with Luke and Louis's share of chicken, salad vegetables and dressing. In Sarah's separate bowl, fold her gluten-free fusilli with her saved share of chicken, salad vegetables and dressing. Plate by the finished serving elements rather than guessing from the mixed bowl.

Uses:

- Finished serving elements - as shown in the Plate table

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Finished chicken pasta salad bowls | about 43% of the chicken, salad veg and dressing, plus about 70% of the normal pasta branch | about 33% of the chicken, salad veg and dressing, plus the Sarah gluten-free pasta branch | about 24% of the chicken, salad veg and dressing, plus about 30% of the normal pasta branch |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 688.9 kcal, 59.6g protein, 76.7g carbs, 16.4g fat, GBP 2.01
- Sarah: 517.4 kcal, 45.4g protein, 59.3g carbs, 12.5g fat, GBP 1.57
- Louis: 342.1 kcal, 31.9g protein, 33.9g carbs, 8.9g fat, GBP 1.09

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Cottage Cheese Scrambled Eggs on Toast

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 12 minutes

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Carbs

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 180g

### Protein

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 6 each
- Everyday Essentials Low Fat Cottage Cheese - 180g

### Dairy

- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 20g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- single oven
- mixing bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: Set out 3 slices of white sourdough and keep 15g butter ready for the hot toast.
- Cottage egg jug: Crack in 6 eggs, add 180g cottage cheese, then season with a small pinch of salt and black pepper if wanted.

## Method

### 1. Toast and butter the sourdough

Use the Toast board. Toast the 3 sourdough slices until golden and firm. Butter each hot slice with 5g butter so it melts in. This should take about 4 minutes.

Uses:

- White sourdough toast - amount stated in this step: Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf 180g
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 20g

### 2. Mix the cottage egg jug

Use the Cottage egg jug. Crack in the 6 eggs, add the 180g cottage cheese, then whisk briefly so the eggs break up and the cottage cheese is evenly dotted through. Add a pinch of salt and black pepper if wanted.

Uses:

- Cottage cheese scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 6 each, Everyday Essentials Low Fat Cottage Cheese 180g

### 3. Soft scramble the cottage eggs

Melt 5g butter in the 32 cm tri-ply saute pan over low heat. Pour in the Cottage egg jug and slowly scrape the bottom up with the spatula for about 4-5 minutes until the eggs are softly set and still glossy.

Uses:

- Cottage cheese scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 6 each, Everyday Essentials Low Fat Cottage Cheese 180g
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 20g

### 4. Portion onto the toast

Take the eggs off the heat while they are still soft. Spoon about 40% of the cooked egg batch onto Luke's toast, about 35% onto Sarah's toast and about 25% onto Louis's toast. This should take about 1 minute.

Uses:

- Cottage cheese scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 6 each, Everyday Essentials Low Fat Cottage Cheese 180g
- White sourdough toast - amount stated in this step: Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf 180g

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Buttered sourdough toast | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice |
| Cottage cheese scrambled eggs | about 40% of the cottage egg batch | about 35% of the cottage egg batch | about 25% of the cottage egg batch |

- Sarah: 384.3 kcal, 26.3g protein, 30.3g carbs, 18.5g fat, GBP 0.88

# Creamy Garlic Chicken And Broccoli

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 38 minutes

Meal role: dinner

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Chicken breast fillets - 490g

### Carbs

- British white potatoes - 510g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick British Broccoli - 295g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Brown onions - 110g
- Lighter soft cheese - 60g
- Parmesan cheese - 25g
- Garlic - 12g
- Olive oil - 15ml
- Unsalted butter - 10g
- Smoked paprika - 2g
- Dried oregano - 1.5g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- mixing bowl
- tongs
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Potato pan: Peel if wanted, then cut the potatoes into even chunky pieces so they boil and steam dry at the same pace.
- Broccoli bowl: Cut the fresh broccoli into bite-size florets and weigh 295g into the bowl so it is ready to cook after the potatoes.
- Chicken board: Slice the chicken into bite-size pieces so it browns quickly in one wide pan and stays easy to coat with the finished sauce.
- Onion bowl: Slice 1 medium onion. This goes into the pan first so it can soften before garlic or dairy is added. Used in Step 4.
- Garlic and seasoning station: Crush 4 small garlic cloves. Measure the smoked paprika and oregano straight into the pan when needed because there are only two dried seasonings. Used in Step 4.
- Dairy and cheese bowl: Weigh 60g lighter soft cheese and 25g parmesan together. Keep the 10g butter beside the pan for softening the onion. Used in Step 4 after the onion and garlic have cooked.
- Serve setup: Set out the colander for the potatoes, 3 dinner plates, the probe thermometer and a clean plate for the cooked chicken before the hot steps begin.

## Method

### 1. Boil the potatoes

Cover the potato pan with cold water, bring it to the boil, add salt, then simmer for about 15-18 minutes until the chunks are tender. Drain them in the colander and let them steam dry for about 2 minutes.

Uses:

- Boiled potatoes - all: British white potatoes 510g

### 2. Cook the broccoli

Refill the potato saucepan with fresh boiling water. Tip in the Broccoli bowl and simmer for about 3-4 minutes until bright green and just tender, then drain it well and keep it warm.

Uses:

- Fresh broccoli - all: Nature's Pick British Broccoli 295g

### 3. Brown the chicken

Heat the saute pan over medium-high heat with 10ml olive oil measured straight into the pan. Add the Chicken board pieces in one pan load and cook for about 7-9 minutes, turning as needed, until browned and the thickest piece reaches 75C. Move the chicken to the clean resting plate from the Serve setup.

Uses:

- Creamy garlic chicken pieces - all: Chicken breast fillets 490g
- Creamy garlic onion sauce - amount stated in this step: Brown onions 110g, Lighter soft cheese 60g, Parmesan cheese 25g, Garlic 12g, Olive oil 15ml, Unsalted butter 10g, Smoked paprika 2g, Dried oregano 1.5g

### 4. Soften the onion

Lower the heat to medium. Add 5ml olive oil and 10g butter, then add the Onion bowl and cook for about 6 minutes until soft and sweet.

Uses:

- Creamy garlic onion sauce - amount stated in this step: Brown onions 110g, Lighter soft cheese 60g, Parmesan cheese 25g, Garlic 12g, Olive oil 15ml, Unsalted butter 10g, Smoked paprika 2g, Dried oregano 1.5g

### 5. Cook the garlic and seasonings

Add the crushed garlic, 2g smoked paprika and 1.5g dried oregano from the Garlic and seasoning station. Cook for about 1 minute, stirring, until the garlic smells fragrant and the dried seasonings have warmed through.

Uses:

- Creamy garlic onion sauce - amount stated in this step: Brown onions 110g, Lighter soft cheese 60g, Parmesan cheese 25g, Garlic 12g, Olive oil 15ml, Unsalted butter 10g, Smoked paprika 2g, Dried oregano 1.5g

### 6. Melt the creamy sauce

Add a splash of water to loosen the pan, then stir in the Dairy and cheese bowl for about 2-3 minutes until the sauce is smooth and glossy.

Uses:

- Creamy garlic onion sauce - amount stated in this step: Brown onions 110g, Lighter soft cheese 60g, Parmesan cheese 25g, Garlic 12g, Olive oil 15ml, Unsalted butter 10g, Smoked paprika 2g, Dried oregano 1.5g

### 7. Finish the creamy chicken and plate

Return the chicken to the pan and coat it in the finished sauce for about 1-2 minutes until everything is hot. Plate the creamy chicken, drained potatoes and drained broccoli by the saved finished-batch shares.

Uses:

- Finished serving elements - as shown in the Plate table

## Plate

| Boiled Potatoes | about 47% of the drained potato pan | about 31% of the drained potato pan | about 22% of the drained potato pan |
| Fresh Broccoli | about 46% of the drained broccoli | about 27% of the drained broccoli | about 27% of the drained broccoli |

- Sarah: 457.3 kcal, 48.5g protein, 39.3g carbs, 12.9g fat, GBP 1.80

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Garlic Butter Steak Bites And Potatoes

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 34 minutes

Meal role: dinner

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Beef Medallion Steaks - 340g

### Carbs

- British white potatoes - 575g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick British Broccoli - 300g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Unsalted butter - 20g
- Olive oil - 15ml
- Garlic - 12g
- Fresh flat-leaf parsley - 6g
- Lemon juice - 15g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- mixing bowl
- tongs
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Potato pan: Weigh 575g white potatoes, peel if wanted, then cut into bite-size chunks. Used in Steps 1, 2 and 5.
- Broccoli bowl: Cut the fresh broccoli into bite-size florets and weigh 300g into the bowl. Used in Steps 3 and 5.
- Steak board: Pat the 340g beef medallion steaks dry, then cut into chunky bite-size pieces. Keep raw steak separate from the vegetable prep. Used in Steps 4 and 5.
- Garlic butter finish: Weigh 20g unsalted butter, crush 12g garlic, chop 6g parsley, and halve the lemon ready to squeeze 15g juice into the pan. The 15ml olive oil stays measured straight into the pan during the method. Used in Steps 2 and 4.
- Serve setup: Set out 3 dinner plates, a clean plate for crisped potatoes, a clean plate for steak, tongs, and the probe thermometer before the hot pan work starts. Used in Steps 2, 4 and 5.

## Method

### 1. Parboil the potatoes

Put the Potato pan into the saucepan, cover with cold water, bring to a boil, then simmer for about 8-10 minutes until the outside is soft but the pieces are not falling apart. Drain well and let them steam dry for 2 minutes.

Uses:

- Crisped white potatoes - amount stated in this step: British white potatoes 575g

### 2. Crisp the potatoes

Heat the saute pan over medium-high heat. Add 10ml of the olive oil straight to the pan, then add the drained potatoes in one layer. Cook for about 10-12 minutes, turning every few minutes, until golden on the edges. Lift the potatoes to the clean plate from the Serve setup.

Uses:

- Crisped white potatoes - amount stated in this step: British white potatoes 575g
- Garlic butter parsley finish - amount stated in this step: Unsalted butter 20g, Olive oil 15ml, Garlic 12g, Fresh flat-leaf parsley 6g, Lemon juice 15g

### 3. Cook the broccoli

Refill the potato saucepan with fresh boiling water. Tip in the Broccoli bowl and simmer for about 3-4 minutes until bright green and just tender. Drain well and keep warm for plating.

Uses:

- Fresh broccoli - all: Nature's Pick British Broccoli 300g

### 4. Sear the steak and make the garlic butter

Put the saute pan back over high heat. Add the remaining 5ml olive oil, then add the Steak board pieces in one pan load. Leave them alone for the first minute so they brown, then turn and cook for about 3-4 minutes total. Lower the heat, add the Garlic butter finish butter and garlic, and toss for about 30-45 seconds until glossy. Squeeze in the lemon juice and add the parsley off the heat.

Uses:

- Garlic butter steak bites - all: Ashfields Beef Medallion Steaks 340g
- Garlic butter parsley finish - amount stated in this step: Unsalted butter 20g, Olive oil 15ml, Garlic 12g, Fresh flat-leaf parsley 6g, Lemon juice 15g

### 5. Plate by finished serving elements

Plate the Garlic Butter Steak Bites using the saved steak portions and garlic butter shares: Luke 160g steak, Sarah 135g steak, Louis 45g steak. Add the crisped potatoes and fresh broccoli using their saved finished serving elements.

Uses:

- Finished serving elements - as shown in the Plate table

## Plate

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Greek Chicken Potato Salad Box

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 25 minutes

Meal role: lunch

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets - 480g

### Carbs

- Nature's Pick British Baby Potatoes - 500g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick Cucumber - 150g
- Nature's Pick Cherry Tomatoes - 250g

### Dairy

- Emporium Greek Feta - 70g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 30ml total
- Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons - 50g total
- Nature's Pick Garlic - 6g
- Stonemill Dried Oregano - 2g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- mixing bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Potato pan: Weigh 500g baby potatoes and halve only any large ones so they cook evenly. Used in Steps 1, 4 and 5.
- Chicken board: Cut 480g chicken breast into bite-size lunch-box pieces. Used in Steps 2, 3 and 5.
- Chicken coating bowl: Mix 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil, juice from 1/2 lemon, 6g garlic and 1 tsp (2g) dried oregano. Used in Steps 2 and 3.
- Salad board: Chop 150g cucumber and 250g cherry tomatoes, then crumble 70g feta. Used in Steps 4 and 5.
- Salad dressing bowl: Mix 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil with juice from 1/2 lemon. Used in Step 4.
- Lunch box setup: Set out 3 lunch boxes for the finished portions. Used in Step 5.

## Method

### 1. Cook the baby potatoes

Use the Potato pan. Cover the potatoes with water, bring to a simmer, and cook for about 14-16 minutes until tender. Drain well and leave them uncovered for a few minutes so steam escapes.

Uses:

- Baby potato salad base - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick British Baby Potatoes 500g

### 2. Coat the chicken

Add the Chicken board to the Chicken coating bowl and turn for about 1 minute until every piece is lightly coated. This is a quick coating for cooking, not a long marinade.

Uses:

- Greek lemon oregano chicken - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g
- Lemon oregano chicken seasoning - amount stated in this step: Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 25g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g, Stonemill Dried Oregano 2g

### 3. Sear the chicken

Heat the 32 cm tri-ply saute pan over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned chicken in one layer and cook for about 7-8 minutes, turning occasionally, until lightly browned and cooked through. Tip it onto a clean plate to cool slightly.

Uses:

- Greek lemon oregano chicken - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g
- Lemon oregano chicken seasoning - amount stated in this step: Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 25g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g, Stonemill Dried Oregano 2g

### 4. Dress the potato salad

Use the Salad board and Salad dressing bowl. Fold the drained potatoes with the cucumber, tomatoes, feta and dressing. Keep it loose and lightly coated rather than mashed.

Uses:

- Baby potato salad base - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick British Baby Potatoes 500g
- Cucumber and tomato salad - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Cucumber 150g, Nature's Pick Cherry Tomatoes 250g
- Feta for Greek salad box - amount stated in this step: Emporium Greek Feta 70g
- Lemon olive oil salad dressing - all: Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 25g

### 5. Pack the lunch boxes

Use the Lunch box setup. Portion about 43% of the chicken and potato salad into Luke's box, about 33% into Sarah's box and about 24% into Louis's box. Chill if not eating straight away.

Uses:

- Greek lemon oregano chicken - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g
- Baby potato salad base - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick British Baby Potatoes 500g
- Cucumber and tomato salad - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Cucumber 150g, Nature's Pick Cherry Tomatoes 250g
- Feta for Greek salad box - amount stated in this step: Emporium Greek Feta 70g

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Finished Greek chicken potato salad box | about 43% of the cooked chicken, potatoes, salad, feta and dressing | about 33% of the cooked chicken, potatoes, salad, feta and dressing | about 24% of the cooked chicken, potatoes, salad, feta and dressing |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 595.7 kcal, 60g protein, 41.2g carbs, 22.4g fat, GBP 2.77
- Sarah: 457.1 kcal, 46.1g protein, 31.4g carbs, 17.2g fat, GBP 2.14
- Louis: 332.4 kcal, 33.5g protein, 22.9g carbs, 12.5g fat, GBP 1.53

# Greek Yoghurt Oat Bowl with Berries and Seeds

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 12 minutes

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt - 730g

### Carbs

- Everyday Essentials Porridge Oats - 105g

### Fruit

- Nature's Pick Strawberries - 225g

### Pantry

- Chia seeds - 23g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Everyday Essentials Clear Honey - 15g

## Equipment

- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Bowl set: Set 3 breakfast bowls on the scales with the yoghurt tub, oats and spoon ready.
- Fruit board: Wash, hull and slice 225g fresh strawberries.
- Finish station: Keep chia seeds and honey beside the bowls to measure directly into each bowl.

## Method

### 1. Build the yoghurt oat bases

Use the Bowl set. Spoon yoghurt into the bowls, then stir oats through each bowl and leave them for about 5 minutes so the oats soften. Luke gets 280g yoghurt and 45g oats, Sarah gets 270g yoghurt and 35g oats, and Louis gets 180g yoghurt and 25g oats.

Uses:

- Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt - all: Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt 730g
- Everyday Essentials Porridge Oats - all: Everyday Essentials Porridge Oats 105g

### 2. Prepare and add the strawberries

Use the Fruit board. Wash, hull and slice the strawberries, then add them to the bowls. Luke gets 85g strawberries, Sarah gets 80g and Louis gets 60g.

Uses:

- Nature's Pick Strawberries - all: Nature's Pick Strawberries 225g

### 3. Finish with chia and honey

Use the Finish station. Measure chia and honey directly onto each bowl. Luke gets 10g chia and 5g honey, Sarah gets 8g chia and 5g honey, and Louis gets 5g chia and 5g honey. Serve chilled.

Uses:

- Chia seeds - all: Chia seeds 23g
- Everyday Essentials Clear Honey - all: Everyday Essentials Clear Honey 15g

## Plate

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Lemon Paprika Chicken Couscous Lunch Box

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 30 minutes

Meal role: lunch

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets - 480g

### Carbs

- Worldwide Foods Couscous - 210g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick Cucumber - 210g
- Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers - 160g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 15ml
- Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons - 45g total
- Stonemill Smoked Paprika - 4g
- Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt - 120g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- mixing bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards
- 3 lunch boxes
- 3 small sauce pots

## Mise En Place

- Chicken board: Cut 480g chicken breast into bite-size pieces. Used in Steps 3, 4 and 7.
- Couscous bowl: Weigh 210g dry couscous into a mixing bowl. Used in Step 2.
- Salad board: Dice 210g cucumber and 1 mixed pepper. Used in Steps 5 and 7.
- Sauce bowl: Stir 120g fat-free Greek yoghurt with 20g lemon juice. Used in Step 6.
- Pack setup: Set out 3 lunch boxes and 3 small sauce pots. Used in Step 7.

## Method

### 1. Prep the lunch-box stations

Set up the Chicken board, Couscous bowl, Salad board, Sauce bowl and Pack setup before cooking starts. This is prep only and should take about 7 minutes.

Uses:

- Lemon paprika chicken pieces - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g
- Lemon couscous - amount stated in this step: Worldwide Foods Couscous 210g
- Cucumber and pepper crunch - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Cucumber 210g, Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 160g
- Lemon yoghurt sauce on the side - amount stated in this step: Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt 120g, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 20g

### 2. Hydrate the couscous

Pour about 250ml boiling water over the Couscous bowl, cover it, and leave it for about 5 minutes. Fluff it with a fork, then spread it out a little so it cools instead of steaming in a clump.

Uses:

- Lemon couscous - amount stated in this step: Worldwide Foods Couscous 210g

### 3. Coat the chicken

Add the Chicken board to a mixing bowl with 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil, 25g lemon juice and 4g smoked paprika. Turn for about 1 minute until every piece is lightly coated. This is a quick coating, not a long marinade.

Uses:

- Lemon paprika chicken pieces - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g
- Lemon paprika chicken coating - all: Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 25g, Stonemill Smoked Paprika 4g

### 4. Sear the chicken

Heat the 32 cm tri-ply saute pan over medium-high heat. Cook the coated chicken in one pan load for about 7-8 minutes, turning every couple of minutes, until lightly browned and cooked through. Lift it onto a clean plate to cool.

Uses:

- Lemon paprika chicken pieces - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g

### 5. Finish the salad crunch

Use the Salad board. Keep the cucumber and pepper pieces dry and separate until the chicken and couscous have cooled, so the lunch box stays fresh.

Uses:

- Cucumber and pepper crunch - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Cucumber 210g, Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 160g

### 6. Mix the side sauce

Stir the Sauce bowl until smooth. Keep it in small sauce pots rather than tipping it over the lunch boxes now.

Uses:

- Lemon yoghurt sauce on the side - amount stated in this step: Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt 120g, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 20g

### 7. Pack the finished boxes

When the chicken and couscous are no longer steaming, pack the finished chicken couscous box by the saved plate guide: Luke about 43%, Sarah about 33% and Louis about 24%. Add the lemon yoghurt sauce as separate side pots using the same shares.

Uses:

- Lemon paprika chicken pieces - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g
- Lemon couscous - amount stated in this step: Worldwide Foods Couscous 210g
- Cucumber and pepper crunch - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Cucumber 210g, Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 160g
- Lemon yoghurt sauce on the side - amount stated in this step: Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt 120g, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 20g

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Finished lemon paprika chicken couscous box | about 43% of the cooked chicken, couscous, cucumber and peppers | about 33% of the cooked chicken, couscous, cucumber and peppers | about 24% of the cooked chicken, couscous, cucumber and peppers |
| Lemon yoghurt sauce pot | about 43% of the lemon yoghurt sauce pot | about 33% of the lemon yoghurt sauce pot | about 24% of the lemon yoghurt sauce pot |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 666.8 kcal, 65g protein, 80.4g carbs, 11.6g fat, GBP 2.38
- Sarah: 511.8 kcal, 49.9g protein, 61.6g carbs, 9g fat, GBP 1.82
- Louis: 372.3 kcal, 36.2g protein, 44.8g carbs, 6.6g fat, GBP 1.31

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Mexican Chicken And Rice

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 40 minutes

Meal role: dinner

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets - 460g

### Carbs

- Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice - 140g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers - 160g
- Nature's Pick Brown Onions - 55g
- Four Seasons Sweetcorn - 60g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Everyday Essentials Chopped Tomatoes in Tomato Juice - 400g
- Cucina Double Concentrate Tomato Puree - 25g
- Olive oil - 15ml
- Garlic - 9g
- Stonemill Smoked Paprika - 4g
- Stonemill Ground Cumin - 4g
- Stonemill Dried Oregano - 2g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- mixing bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Chicken board: Cut 460g chicken breast into bite-size pieces and keep it separate from vegetable prep. Used in Steps 1, 4 and 5.
- Pepper and onion bowl: Slice 1 mixed pepper and 1/2 medium onion. Keep 60g frozen sweetcorn separate in the freezer until Step 3 so it does not water out the bowl. Used in Steps 2, 3 and 5.
- Mild spice pot: Measure the smoked paprika, cumin and oregano together because this is three or more dried spices going into the pan at the same fast step. Crush 3 small garlic cloves separately beside the pot. Used in Step 2.
- Tomato and bean bowl: Open the chopped tomatoes, drain and rinse the kidney beans, and keep 25g tomato puree ready at the side. The tomato puree goes in first; tomatoes and beans go in later. Used in Steps 2 and 3.
- Rice cup: Weigh 140g long grain white rice. Rinse only if you normally do. Used in Step 3.
- Serve setup: Set out 3 dinner plates and a clean resting plate for the browned chicken. Used in Steps 1 and 5.

## Method

### 1. Brown the chicken

Heat the saute pan over medium-high heat. Add 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil straight to the pan, then add the Chicken board in one pan load. Cook for about 5-6 minutes, turning once or twice, until the outside is lightly browned. Lift the chicken to the clean resting plate from the Serve setup.

Uses:

- Mild Mexican chicken pieces - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 460g
- Mild Mexican seasoning - amount stated in this step: Olive oil 15ml, Garlic 9g, Stonemill Smoked Paprika 4g, Stonemill Ground Cumin 4g, Stonemill Dried Oregano 2g

### 2. Soften vegetables and bloom the flavour

Tip in the Pepper and onion bowl, but keep the frozen sweetcorn back for the next step. Cook for about 6-7 minutes until the onion is softened and the peppers have lost their raw edge. Add the crushed garlic, Mild spice pot and 25g tomato puree, then cook for about 1 minute so the garlic and spices smell cooked rather than raw.

Uses:

- Pepper, onion and sweetcorn mix - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 160g, Nature's Pick Brown Onions 55g, Four Seasons Sweetcorn 60g
- Mild Mexican seasoning - amount stated in this step: Olive oil 15ml, Garlic 9g, Stonemill Smoked Paprika 4g, Stonemill Ground Cumin 4g, Stonemill Dried Oregano 2g
- Tomato and kidney bean base - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Chopped Tomatoes in Tomato Juice 400g, Cucina Double Concentrate Tomato Puree 25g

### 3. Build the rice pan

Stir in the Rice cup, the chopped tomatoes and drained kidney beans from the Tomato and bean bowl, 60g frozen sweetcorn and about 280ml water. Bring it to a steady simmer for about 2 minutes, scraping the bottom so the tomato puree does not catch.

Uses:

- Long grain white rice - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 140g
- Pepper, onion and sweetcorn mix - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 160g, Nature's Pick Brown Onions 55g, Four Seasons Sweetcorn 60g
- Tomato and kidney bean base - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Chopped Tomatoes in Tomato Juice 400g, Cucina Double Concentrate Tomato Puree 25g

### 4. Simmer until the rice is cooked

Return the browned chicken and any resting juices to the pan. Put the lid on, reduce to low, and simmer for about 16-18 minutes until the rice is tender and the chicken is cooked through. If the pan looks dry before the rice is tender, add a small splash of water and keep the lid on.

Uses:

- Mild Mexican chicken pieces - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 460g
- Long grain white rice - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 140g
- Pepper, onion and sweetcorn mix - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 160g, Nature's Pick Brown Onions 55g, Four Seasons Sweetcorn 60g
- Tomato and kidney bean base - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Chopped Tomatoes in Tomato Juice 400g, Cucina Double Concentrate Tomato Puree 25g
- Mild Mexican seasoning - amount stated in this step: Olive oil 15ml, Garlic 9g, Stonemill Smoked Paprika 4g, Stonemill Ground Cumin 4g, Stonemill Dried Oregano 2g

### 5. Rest and plate the finished pan

Turn the heat off and rest the pan with the lid on for about 5 minutes so the rice settles. Fluff and fold the pan together, then plate the finished chicken rice pan by the saved serving element: Luke about 43%, Sarah about 33% and Louis about 24%.

Uses:

- Finished serving elements - as shown in the Plate table

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Finished Mexican Chicken And Rice Pan | about 43% of the finished chicken rice pan | about 33% of the finished chicken rice pan | about 24% of the finished chicken rice pan |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 573.3 kcal, 56.5g protein, 67.9g carbs, 10.8g fat, GBP 2.30
- Sarah: 439.9 kcal, 43.4g protein, 52.1g carbs, 8.4g fat, GBP 1.75
- Louis: 319.9 kcal, 31.7g protein, 37.9g carbs, 5.9g fat, GBP 1.28

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttered Poached Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Soft poached eggs on hot buttered white sourdough, seasoned simply at the plate.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 25 minutes

Yield: 3 plates

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 eggs
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 15g total

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 3 slices, about 180g total

## Equipment

- 20 cm saucepan with lid
- Slotted spoon
- Toaster
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: 3 slices of white sourdough with 15g unsalted butter ready beside them. Used in Step 2.
- Egg cups: 8 eggs, cracked into cups if you want cleaner poaching, or kept whole if cracking straight into the water. Used in Step 3.
- Finish station: salt and black pepper beside the plates. Used in Step 4.

## Method

### 1. Heat the poaching water

Bring the saucepan of water to a gentle simmer, then lower the heat so the water is moving but not bubbling hard. This usually takes about 5 minutes.

Uses:

- Water - enough for poaching

### 2. Toast and butter the sourdough

Toast the sourdough until golden and firm. Butter each slice while it is hot so the butter melts into the toast. This should take about 4 minutes.

Uses:

- Toast board - all: 3 slices white sourdough and 15g unsalted butter

### 3. Poach the eggs

Slide the eggs into the water in controlled rounds if the pan is crowded. Cook gently until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft. Allow about 3-4 minutes per round.

Uses:

- Egg cups - all: 8 eggs

### 4. Plate and season

Lift the eggs out with a slotted spoon and drain briefly. Plate using the table below, then finish each plate with salt and black pepper.

Uses:

- Buttered sourdough toast - all
- Poached eggs - all
- Finish station - salt and black pepper to taste

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Buttered sourdough toast | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice |
| Poached eggs | 3 eggs | 3 eggs | 2 eggs |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 377.2 | 24.4g | 28.3g | 19.3g | GBP 0.86 | 95.1 |
| Sarah | 377.2 | 24.4g | 28.3g | 19.3g | GBP 0.86 | 95.1 |
| Louis | 310.1 | 17.9g | 28.3g | 14.7g | GBP 0.67 | 74.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- Butter the toast as soon as it comes out, then put the eggs on top.
- If the pan feels crowded, poach the eggs in two or three controlled rounds.
- Plate Sarah and Louis first if useful, then finish Luke's 3-egg plate.
- Salt and black pepper are neutral helpers, so they are mentioned in the method but not counted in the shopping or nutrition rows.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Pork Fajita Rice Lunch Box

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 35 minutes

Meal role: lunch

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Lean Pork Fillet - 430g

### Carbs

- Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice - 150g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers - 320g
- Nature's Pick Brown Onions - 110g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Solesta Vegetable Oil - 15ml
- Stonemill Ground Cumin - 4g
- Stonemill Smoked Paprika - 4g
- Nature's Pick Garlic - 6g
- Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack - 30g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Rice cup: Weigh 150g long grain white rice. Used in Steps 1 and 5.
- Pork board: Slice 430g lean pork fillet into thin strips. Used in Steps 2, 4 and 5.
- Fajita veg board: Slice 320g mixed peppers and 1 medium brown onion (110g). Used in Steps 3, 4 and 5.
- Fajita seasoning by hob: Mince 6g garlic. Keep cumin, smoked paprika, 1 tbsp (15ml) oil and 1 lime by the hob to measure straight into the pan. Used in Step 4.
- Pack setup: Set out the lunch boxes for the finished pork fajita rice portions. Used in Step 5.

## Method

### 1. Cook the rice

Put the Rice cup into the saucepan with about 300ml water. Bring to a simmer, cover, cook for about 12 minutes, then turn the heat off and rest for 5 minutes with the lid on.

Uses:

- Long grain white rice - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 150g

### 2. Sear the pork

Heat the saute pan over medium-high heat. Add the Pork board in one wide layer and cook for about 5-6 minutes, turning once or twice, until the strips have browned edges.

Uses:

- Mild pork fajita strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Lean Pork Fillet 430g

### 3. Soften the peppers and onion

Add the Fajita veg board to the pan and cook for about 5 minutes, stirring often, until the onion softens and the peppers still hold their shape.

Uses:

- Fajita peppers and onion - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 320g, Nature's Pick Brown Onions 110g

### 4. Bloom the mild fajita seasoning

Use the Fajita seasoning by hob. Add 1 tbsp (15ml) vegetable oil if the pan looks dry, then add the garlic, 2 tsp (4g) cumin and 2 tsp (4g) smoked paprika straight into the pan. Stir for about 1 minute until the garlic and spices smell cooked, then squeeze in the lime juice and turn everything through.

Uses:

- Mild pork fajita strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Lean Pork Fillet 430g
- Fajita peppers and onion - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 320g, Nature's Pick Brown Onions 110g
- Mild fajita seasoning and lime - amount stated in this step: Solesta Vegetable Oil 15ml, Stonemill Ground Cumin 4g, Stonemill Smoked Paprika 4g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g, Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack 30g

### 5. Cool and pack the lunch boxes

Use the Pack setup. Let the rice and pork fajita mix stop steaming, then spend about 3-4 minutes portioning by the saved finished serving element: Luke about 43%, Sarah about 33% and Louis about 24%. This is a finished-box share, so do not weigh cooked rice or cooked pork as raw ingredient amounts.

Uses:

- Mild pork fajita strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Lean Pork Fillet 430g
- Long grain white rice - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 150g
- Fajita peppers and onion - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 320g, Nature's Pick Brown Onions 110g
- Mild fajita seasoning and lime - amount stated in this step: Solesta Vegetable Oil 15ml, Stonemill Ground Cumin 4g, Stonemill Smoked Paprika 4g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g, Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack 30g

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Finished pork fajita rice lunch box | about 43% of the finished pork fajita rice lunch box | about 33% of the finished pork fajita rice lunch box | about 24% of the finished pork fajita rice lunch box |

- Sarah: 396.5 kcal, 37.6g protein, 49.4g carbs, 6.9g fat, GBP 1.64
- Louis: 288.4 kcal, 27.3g protein, 35.9g carbs, 5g fat, GBP 1.21

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Pork Souvlaki With Greek Salad And Rice

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 25 minutes

Meal role: lunch

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Lean Pork Fillet - 500g

### Carbs

- Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice - 140g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick Cucumber - 150g
- Nature's Pick Cherry Tomatoes - 250g

### Dairy

- Emporium Greek Feta - 80g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 30ml total
- Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons - 50g total
- Nature's Pick Garlic - 6g
- Stonemill Dried Oregano - 2g

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- mixing bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Rice cup: Weigh 140g long grain white rice. Used in Steps 1 and 5.
- Pork board: Slice 500g pork fillet into thin strips and keep it separate from salad prep. Used in Steps 2, 3 and 5.
- Pork marinade bowl: Mix 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil, juice from 1/2 lemon, 6g garlic and 1 tsp (2g) oregano. Used in Steps 2 and 3.
- Greek salad bowl: Chop 150g cucumber and 250g cherry tomatoes, then crumble in 80g feta. Used in Steps 4 and 5.
- Salad dressing jug: Mix 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil with juice from 1/2 lemon. Used in Step 4.
- Serve setup: Set out 3 plates for the finished pork, rice and Greek salad portions. Used in Step 5.

## Method

### 1. Cook the rice

Put the Rice cup into the saucepan with about 280ml water. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for about 12 minutes, then turn the heat off and let it rest with the lid on while the pork finishes.

Uses:

- Long grain white rice - all: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 140g

### 2. Coat the pork

Add the Pork board to the Pork marinade bowl and turn until every strip is lightly coated. This is a quick coating, not an overnight marinade.

Uses:

- Pork souvlaki strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Lean Pork Fillet 500g
- Lemon oregano pork marinade - amount stated in this step: Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 25g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g, Stonemill Dried Oregano 2g

### 3. Sear the pork

Heat the saute pan over medium-high heat. Add the coated pork in one wide layer and cook for about 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned at the edges and cooked through. The oregano and garlic coating should smell cooked, not raw.

Uses:

- Pork souvlaki strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Lean Pork Fillet 500g
- Lemon oregano pork marinade - amount stated in this step: Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 25g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g, Stonemill Dried Oregano 2g

### 4. Dress the Greek salad

Pour the Salad dressing jug over the Greek salad bowl and fold gently so the cucumber, tomatoes and feta stay distinct rather than smashed.

Uses:

- Greek salad vegetables - all: Nature's Pick Cucumber 150g, Nature's Pick Cherry Tomatoes 250g
- Feta for Greek salad - all: Emporium Greek Feta 80g
- Lemon olive oil salad dressing - all: Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil 15ml, Everyday Essentials Wonky Lemons 25g

### 5. Plate the pork, rice and salad

Use the Serve setup. Fluff the rice, rest the pork for 2 minutes, then plate the finished pork, rice and Greek salad by the saved serving element: Luke about 43%, Sarah about 33% and Louis about 24%.

Uses:

- Finished serving elements - as shown in the Plate table

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttery Scrambled Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 18 minutes

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Carbs

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 180g

### Protein

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 each

### Dairy

- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 23g

### Finish


## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- jug
- fork or small whisk
- silicone spatula
- toaster
- plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: Set out 3 slices of white sourdough and keep 15g of the butter ready for the toast.
- Egg jug: Crack 8 eggs into a jug, add a light pinch of salt and a light grind of black pepper per person, then whisk until the eggs are mostly combined.
- Pan butter dish: Keep 8g butter ready by the warmed saute pan so it can melt just before the egg jug goes in.

## Method

### 1. Warm the saute pan

Put the 32 cm tri-ply saute pan on the lowest heat for about 2-3 minutes so the base warms gently without browning the butter later.

### 2. Whisk the eggs

Crack the 8 eggs into the Egg jug, add the measured salt and black pepper, then whisk for about 1 minute until the yolks and whites are mostly combined.

Uses:

- Soft scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 8 each

### 3. Toast and butter the bread

Use the Toast board: put the 3 sourdough slices in the toaster. When they are done, butter each hot slice with 5g of the toast butter. This should take about 4 minutes.

Uses:

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - amount stated in this step: Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf 180g
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 23g

### 4. Cook the eggs softly

Use the Pan butter dish: melt 8g butter in the warm pan, pour in the Egg jug, then slowly scrape the bottom up with the spatula for about 4-5 minutes until the eggs are almost cooked but still soft.

Uses:

- Soft scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 8 each
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 23g

### 5. Finish off the heat

Take the pan off the heat while the eggs are still slightly glossy. Finish the toast, then spoon the scrambled eggs over the buttered slices by finished serving element: Luke and Sarah get about two fifths each, and Louis gets about one quarter. This should take about 1 minute.

Uses:

- Soft scrambled eggs - amount stated in this step: Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs 8 each
- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - amount stated in this step: Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf 180g
- Unsalted butter for toast and pan - amount stated in this step: Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter 23g

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Buttered Sourdough Toast | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice |
| Soft Scrambled Eggs | about two fifths of the cooked egg batch | about two fifths of the cooked egg batch | about one quarter of the cooked egg batch |

- Sarah: 413.8 kcal, 25.8g protein, 28.2g carbs, 22.9g fat, GBP 0.87
- Louis: 334.3 kcal, 18.9g protein, 28.2g carbs, 17.1g fat, GBP 0.68

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Soy Ginger Chicken Noodle Salad

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 29 minutes

Meal role: lunch

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets - 480g

### Carbs

- Instant Noodles - 170g

### Vegetables

- Nature's Pick Cucumber - 210g
- Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers - 160g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Asia Specialities Dark Soy Sauce - 30ml
- Nature's Pick Ginger - 10g
- Everyday Essentials Clear Honey - 15g
- Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack - 25g
- Solesta Vegetable Oil - 15ml

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- mixing bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards
- 3 lunch boxes

## Mise En Place

- Noodle bowl: Use 2 packs (170g) instant noodles and discard seasoning sachets if present. Used in Steps 2 and 6.
- Chicken board: Cut 480g chicken breast into bite-size pieces. Used in Steps 3 and 6.
- Soy ginger dressing bowl: Mix 2 tbsp (30ml) soy sauce, 10g grated ginger, 1 tbsp (15g) honey, juice from 1/2 lime and 1 tbsp (15ml) vegetable oil. Used in Steps 4 and 6.
- Salad board: Dice 210g cucumber and 1 mixed pepper. Used in Steps 5 and 6.
- Pack setup: Set out 3 lunch boxes for the finished portions. Used in Step 6.

## Method

### 1. Prep the stations

Set up the Noodle bowl, Chicken board, Soy ginger dressing bowl, Salad board and Pack setup before cooking starts. This should take about 7 minutes.

Uses:

- Cold noodles - amount stated in this step: Instant Noodles 170g
- Soy ginger chicken pieces - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g
- Soy ginger dressing - amount stated in this step: Asia Specialities Dark Soy Sauce 30ml, Nature's Pick Ginger 10g, Everyday Essentials Clear Honey 15g, Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack 25g, Solesta Vegetable Oil 15ml
- Cucumber and pepper crunch - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Cucumber 210g, Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 160g

### 2. Hydrate and cool the noodles

Cover the Noodle bowl with boiling water and leave for about 4 minutes, or until tender. Drain well, rinse briefly with cold water, then leave to drain so the salad does not become watery.

Uses:

- Cold noodles - amount stated in this step: Instant Noodles 170g

### 3. Sear the chicken

Heat the 32 cm tri-ply saute pan over medium-high heat. Cook the Chicken board in one pan load for about 7-8 minutes, turning every couple of minutes, until lightly browned and cooked through. Lift it onto a clean plate to cool.

Uses:

- Soy ginger chicken pieces - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g

### 4. Mix the dressing

Stir the Soy ginger dressing bowl until the honey loosens into the soy, lime, ginger and oil.

Uses:

- Soy ginger dressing - amount stated in this step: Asia Specialities Dark Soy Sauce 30ml, Nature's Pick Ginger 10g, Everyday Essentials Clear Honey 15g, Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack 25g, Solesta Vegetable Oil 15ml

### 5. Finish the crunch

Use the Salad board. Keep the cucumber and pepper pieces dry and cold so they stay crunchy in the lunch boxes.

Uses:

- Cucumber and pepper crunch - amount stated in this step: Nature's Pick Cucumber 210g, Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers 160g

### 6. Fold and pack the finished noodle salad

When the chicken and noodles are no longer steaming, fold everything together with the Soy ginger dressing bowl. Pack by the saved plate guide: Luke about 43%, Sarah about 33% and Louis about 24%.

Uses:

- Soy ginger chicken pieces - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Chicken Breast Fillets 480g

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Sticky Beef Green Bean Rice Box

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

Time: 35 minutes

Meal role: lunch

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Ashfields Beef Medallion Steaks - 400g

### Carbs

- Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice - 150g

### Vegetables

- Four Seasons Whole Green Beans - 300g

### Sauce And Pantry

- Asia Specialities Dark Soy Sauce - 30ml
- Everyday Essentials Clear Honey - 20g
- Nature's Pick Ginger - 10g
- Nature's Pick Garlic - 6g
- Toasted Sesame Oil - 5ml
- Solesta Vegetable Oil - 10ml

## Equipment

- 32 cm tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- small bowl
- basic utensils and chopping boards

## Mise En Place

- Rice cup: Weigh 150g long grain white rice. Used in Steps 1 and 5.
- Beef board: Slice 400g beef medallions into thin strips. Used in Steps 2, 4 and 5.
- Green bean bag: Measure 300g frozen whole green beans. Used in Steps 3, 4 and 5.
- Soy garlic glaze bowl: Mix 30ml soy sauce, 1 tbsp (20g) honey, 10g grated ginger, 6g garlic and 5ml toasted sesame oil. Keep the 10ml vegetable oil by the hob. Used in Step 4.
- Pack setup: Set out the lunch boxes for the finished sticky beef rice portions. Used in Step 5.

## Method

### 1. Cook the rice

Put the Rice cup into the saucepan with about 300ml water. Bring to a simmer, cover, cook for about 12 minutes, then turn the heat off and rest for 5 minutes with the lid on.

Uses:

- Long grain white rice - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 150g

### 2. Sear the beef

Heat the saute pan over medium-high heat. Add 10ml vegetable oil, then add the Beef board in one wide layer and sear for about 3-4 minutes until the strips have browned edges.

Uses:

- Sticky beef strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Beef Medallion Steaks 400g
- Soy garlic ginger glaze - amount stated in this step: Asia Specialities Dark Soy Sauce 30ml, Everyday Essentials Clear Honey 20g, Nature's Pick Ginger 10g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g, Toasted Sesame Oil 5ml, Solesta Vegetable Oil 10ml

### 3. Cook the green beans

Add the Green bean bag to the pan and cook for about 4-5 minutes, stirring often, until the beans are hot through and the freezer steam has cooked off.

Uses:

- Whole green beans - amount stated in this step: Four Seasons Whole Green Beans 300g

### 4. Glaze the beef and beans

Use the Soy garlic glaze bowl. Pour it into the hot pan and bubble for about 1-2 minutes, stirring, until the glaze looks glossy and coats the beef and beans instead of pooling.

Uses:

- Sticky beef strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Beef Medallion Steaks 400g
- Whole green beans - amount stated in this step: Four Seasons Whole Green Beans 300g
- Soy garlic ginger glaze - amount stated in this step: Asia Specialities Dark Soy Sauce 30ml, Everyday Essentials Clear Honey 20g, Nature's Pick Ginger 10g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g, Toasted Sesame Oil 5ml, Solesta Vegetable Oil 10ml

### 5. Cool and pack the lunch boxes

Use the Pack setup. Let the rice and sticky beef mix stop steaming, then spend about 3-4 minutes portioning by the saved finished serving element: Luke about 43%, Sarah about 33% and Louis about 24%. This is a finished-box share, so do not weigh cooked rice or cooked beef as raw ingredient amounts.

Uses:

- Sticky beef strips - amount stated in this step: Ashfields Beef Medallion Steaks 400g
- Long grain white rice - amount stated in this step: Everyday Essentials Long Grain White Rice 150g
- Whole green beans - amount stated in this step: Four Seasons Whole Green Beans 300g
- Soy garlic ginger glaze - amount stated in this step: Asia Specialities Dark Soy Sauce 30ml, Everyday Essentials Clear Honey 20g, Nature's Pick Ginger 10g, Nature's Pick Garlic 6g, Toasted Sesame Oil 5ml, Solesta Vegetable Oil 10ml

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Finished sticky beef green bean rice box | about 43% of the finished sticky beef green bean rice box | about 33% of the finished sticky beef green bean rice box | about 24% of the finished sticky beef green bean rice box |
